sys.unlabeled_deny: Add stable rule labels to deny rules.
Ensure each deny rule emits a label and message.
ops.breakglass_used: Prefer change-approved tags for non-emergency production changes.
terraform.sg_open_world: Restrict ingress CIDR to specific IP ranges instead of 0.0.0.0/0.
Use a bastion host or VPN for SSH/RDP access.
terraform.s3_public_access: Enable all four S3 Block Public Access settings.
Add risk_tag: approved_public if bucket must be public.
terraform.iam_wildcard_policy: Scope IAM Action and Resource to specific services and ARNs.
Use least-privilege policies instead of wildcards.
k8s.privileged_container: Remove securityContext.privileged or set it to false.
Use specific capabilities instead of privileged mode.
k8s.host_namespace_escape: Remove hostPID, hostIPC, and hostNetwork from the pod spec.
Use CNI plugins or service meshes for network access.
k8s.run_as_root: Set securityContext.runAsUser to a non-zero UID.
Add USER directive to Dockerfile to avoid running as root.
k8s.hostpath_mount: Use PersistentVolumeClaims instead of hostPath volumes.
Restrict hostPath usage to infrastructure DaemonSets only.
k8s.dangerous_capabilities: Remove SYS_ADMIN, SYS_PTRACE, and NET_RAW from capabilities.add.
Use capabilities.drop: [ALL] and add back only what is needed.
k8s.mutable_image_tag: Pin container images to a specific version tag or SHA digest.
k8s.no_resource_limits: Set resources.limits.memory and resources.limits.cpu on all containers.
Memory limits prevent OOMKill cascading across co-located pods.
argocd.autosync_prod: Disable automated sync for production Applications.
Use manual sync with approval gates for production deployments.
argocd.wildcard_destination: Specify explicit namespace and server targets in AppProject destinations.
Do not use the default AppProject for production workloads.
argocd.dangerous_sync_combo: Disable selfHeal or prune on production Applications with auto-sync.
Use manual sync for production to retain rollback capability.
aws_s3.no_encryption: Enable server-side encryption (SSE-S3 or SSE-KMS) on the bucket.
aws_s3.no_versioning_prod: Enable versioning on production S3 buckets.
Versioning converts destructive deletes into recoverable soft-deletes.
aws_iam.wildcard_policy: Remove Action:* and Resource:* from IAM policy statements.
Scope permissions to specific services, actions, and resource ARNs.
aws_iam.wildcard_principal: Replace Principal:* with specific AWS account IDs or service principals.
Add Condition keys to restrict who can assume the role.
ops.terraform_metadata_only: Payload contains plan metadata only (counts, resource_types) — no resource-specific fields.
Ops rules for security groups, IAM, and S3 need: security_group_rules, iam_policy_statements, s3_public_access_block.
Options: use MCP mode (AI agent extracts resource config), adapter v2 (deep extraction), or switch to baseline profile.
ops.insufficient_context: Provide required context in payload for this operation.
For kubectl: include namespace. For workload resources (pod/deployment/statefulset/daemonset/job/cronjob): also include containers[] with image.
For terraform: include at least one semantic detail (resource_types, security_group_rules, iam_policy_statements, etc.).
If this is a newly added tool: add a has_sufficient_context clause in deny_insufficient_context.rego.
ops.unknown_destructive: This tool/operation is not recognized by Evidra policy.
Add it to ops.destructive_operations in policy params, or use a known safe suffix (get, list, describe, plan, etc.).
For emergency use: add risk_tag: breakglass to override (will be logged).
ops.truncated_context: Plan output was truncated and may be incomplete.
Review the full plan manually before applying.
Or reduce plan scope with -target flags.
